text:
(a) When a debt or claim secured by a lien has been satisfied, the lienholder shall, within a reasonable time not to exceed the maximum time allowed by Section 348.408 (Outstanding Balance Information; Payment in Full) or 353.405 (Outstanding Balance Information; Payment in Full) (b), Finance Code, as applicable, execute and deliver to the owner, or the owner’s designee, a discharge of the lien in a manner prescribed by the department.

(b) The owner may submit the discharge and title to the department for a new title.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 501. Certificate of Title Act
##### § 501.115 — Discharge of Lien

(a) When a debt or claim secured by a lien has been satisfied, the lienholder shall, within a reasonable time not to exceed the maximum time allowed by Section 348.408 (Outstanding Balance Information; Payment in Full) or 353.405 (Outstanding Balance Information; Payment in Full) (b), Finance Code, as applicable, execute and deliver to the owner, or the owner’s designee, a discharge of the lien in a manner prescribed by the department.

(b) The owner may submit the discharge and title to the department for a new title.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1296 (H.B.2357), Sec. 54, eff. January 1, 2012.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: When a debt or claim secured by a lien has been satisfied, the lienholder shall, within a reasonable time not to exceed the maximum time allowed by Section 348.408 (Outstanding Balance Information; Payment in Full) or 353.405 (Outstanding Balance Information; Payment in Full) (b), Finance Code, as applicable, execute and deliver to the owner, or the owner’s designee, a discharge of the lien in a manner prescribed by the department.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The owner may submit the discharge and title to the department for a new title.

history.raw: Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1296 (H.B.2357), Sec. 54, eff. January 1, 2012.
